footer.description: Norwegian exploration company focused on sustainable mineral development in the Trøndelag region.

slide1.title: Discovering Minerals
slide1.description: Exploring the depths of Norway's mountains to uncover critical minerals essential for Europe's green transition. Our advanced exploration techniques reveal the hidden treasures within.

slide2.title: Responsible Mining for the
slide2.description: Securing Europe's supply of vital resources. We hold 74 exploration licenses across 1,690 km², focusing on high-value minerals essential for renewable energy and geopolitical stability.

slide3.title: Rare Earth Elements
slide3.description: Exploring rare earth elements essential for the energy transition. Contributing to Europe's mineral independence.

slide4.title: Science-Driven Discovery
slide4.description: Pioneering a new era of mineral exploration in Europe with cutting-edge technology and proven expertise.

stats.title: Key Metrics
stats.heading: Exploration at Scale
stats.description: Norve'Ge Minerals AS focuses on onshore mineral exploration in Norway—more cost-effective than deep-sea alternatives—targeting VMS (Volcanogenic Massive Sulphide) systems and ophiolite complexes. We unlock mineral value from historic mines and future-critical resources, combining traditional mining with advanced water-based mineral extraction, recovering rare elements directly from geothermal mine water. Our operations are powered by renewable energy, including proprietary geothermal power solutions and containerized turbine systems. With a strong commitment to environmental stewardship and innovation, Norve'Ge Minerals supports the shift toward a more sustainable resource economy.

explorationLicenses.label: Exploration Licenses
explorationLicenses.description: Norve'Ge Minerals currently holds 74 mineral rights. This includes 15 mines with Adterra and 2 with Nordveg'r, for a total of 23 mines.

licensedArea.label: km² Licensed Area
licensedArea.description: Norve'Ge Minerals has a total licensed area of 1,600 km², plus an additional 90 km² through Adterra and Nordveg'r.

viableFinds.label: Viable Finds
viableFinds.description: Norve'Ge Minerals has identified 31 economically viable sites.

reeTypes.label: REE Mineral Types
reeTypes.description: Multiple REE discoveries across Norway (Monazite, Bastnäsite, Xenotime, Allanite, Zircon, Fergusonite, Euxenite, Samarskite)

partnerships.label: Strategic Partnerships
partnerships.description: Adterra, Steinkjer Municipality, Steinkjer Utvikling AS

rareEarth.title: Rare Earth Elements
rareEarth.subtitle: Critical Minerals for the Energy Transition
rareEarth.heading: Europe's Race for

dependency.title: The Critical Dependency
dependency.description: Europe is heavily dependent on imports for strategic resources. Renewable energy and 5G infrastructure require large amounts of Copper, Nickel, Lithium, Cobalt, and Rare Earth Elements (REE).

refining.title: Lack of Refining Capacity
refining.description: Europe depends on external sources for rare earth refining, creating significant supply chain vulnerabilities and geopolitical risks.

import.title: Import Dependency
import.description: The EU currently imports 100% of its rare earth elements with no domestic extraction, exposing Europe to supply disruptions.

demand.title: Demand Growth by 2030
demand.description: EU demand for REEs in strategic technologies—electric vehicles, wind turbines, defense systems—is projected to increase 5 times by 2030.

rareEarth.recycling: Recycling alone cannot meet demand. Responsible, domestic mining is crucial for Norway's supply security and independence.

act.title: Critical Raw Materials Act
act.description: A strategic plan to build a secure and resilient domestic supply chain and reduce dependency on third countries for critical raw materials.

projects.title: Our Projects
projects.subtitle: Exploring Norway's Mineral Potential
projects.description: Strategic license holdings in Fensfeltet – the world's largest rare earth deposit in Norway.

blockchain.title: Blockchain Technology
blockchain.subtitle: Transparency in Mineral Tracking
blockchain.heading: The Malm Mine
blockchain.description: Turning Environmental Liability into Sustainable Assets
blockchain.description2: We are revolutionizing extraction by utilizing 7–10 million m³ of 26°C ion-rich mine water from the flooded Malm mines. By applying Selective Ion Exchange (IX) and Electro-winning (EW), we extract valuable metals while removing heavy metals from highly contaminated water, significantly reducing emissions compared to conventional mining.
blockchain.description3: Combined with a thermo powerplant based on Double Flash technology (similar to Hellisheiði, Iceland), this creates a self-sustainable mineral processing process. The hot purified water is heated to 120°C using heat exchangers for power generation, then cooled and recirculated in a closed-loop system.

sustainable.title: Sustainable
sustainable.description: Reduces CO₂ emissions by 20–40 tons per kg of minerals compared to traditional mining.

efficient.title: Efficient
efficient.description: Recovers 750–1,500 kg of minerals per cycle.

norchain.title: NorChain
norchain.subtitle: Blockchain-Powered Mineral Tracking
norchain.heading: Our Assets are Tokenized on
norchain.description: Norvege Minerals has partnered with NorChain to bring transparency and accessibility to mineral exploration—enabling secure, verifiable ownership and investment opportunities powered by blockchain technology.

tokenized.title: Tokenized Assets
tokenized.description: Our mineral assets are securely tokenized on the NorChain blockchain for transparent ownership.

global.title: Global Access
global.description: Enabling worldwide investment opportunities in Norwegian mineral exploration with compliant infrastructure.

secure.title: Secure & Transparent
secure.description: Blockchain-verified transactions with complete audit trails and stronger governance for stakeholders.

card.title: Transparent ownership with compliant infrastructure
card.description: A clean on-chain record of assets, governance and audit trails—designed for institutional-grade access and reporting.

contactStrip.description: Connect with our team to discuss exploration projects, investment opportunities, or partnership possibilities.
contactStrip.formDescription: Fill out the form and our team will respond promptly.

hero.title: About
hero.description: Building Norway's mineral future through responsible exploration and sustainable development

overview.title: Company Overview
overview.storySubtitle: A Norwegian mining company committed to responsible exploration and sustainable development
overview.description: Norve'Ge Minerals AS is a Norwegian exploration company focused on unlocking mineral value from historic mines and future-critical resources. We focus on mineral exploration in VMS (Volcanogenic Massive Sulphide) systems on the Norwegian mainland—in areas such as Killingdal and known ophiolite complexes along the coast—where exploration is significantly more cost-effective than in deep-sea settings. We combine traditional mining with advanced water-based mineral extraction, recovering rare elements directly from geothermal mine water. Our operations are powered by renewable energy, including proprietary geothermal power solutions and containerized turbine systems. With a strong commitment to environmental stewardship and innovation, Norve'Ge Minerals supports the shift toward a more sustainable resource economy.

richHeritage.title: Rich Heritage
richHeritage.description: Norway has a rich mining heritage dating back centuries. Today, we stand at the forefront of a new era—one where critical minerals like copper, nickel, cobalt, and rare earth elements are essential for batteries, renewable energy systems, and the electrification of society.

explorationPortfolio.title: Exploration Portfolio
explorationPortfolio.description: Our exploration portfolio spans multiple regions across Norway, targeting both historical mining districts with modern exploration techniques and greenfield opportunities in underexplored areas.

europeanContext.title: European Context
europeanContext.description: As Europe seeks to reduce dependence on imported critical minerals, Norway's stable jurisdiction and mineral potential position us as a strategic domestic supplier.

mineralGrade.title: Mineral Grade
mineralGrade.subtitle: Typical grade ranges for target minerals in our exploration projects

futurePlans.title: Future Plans & Strategic Vision
futurePlans.subtitle: 2025-2029 Roadmap
futurePlans.intro: A comprehensive approach to responsible mining and sustainable development

greenMining.title: Green Mining Strategy
greenMining.description1: Green mining will reduce the footprint on the environment in the future. Our strategy is to use underground operations, which reduces natural encroachment, emissions and land requirements for disposal.
greenMining.description2: While there will be a need for landfill, by thinking about backfilling of rock caverns and using new technology for increased use of secondary resources for crushed stone production and fines in concrete and asphalt production, it is possible to reduce this considerably.

longTermVision.title: Long-term Vision
longTermVision.description: To become a leading supplier of critical minerals in Europe, supporting the continent's transition to renewable energy and electrified transportation.

2025-2027.title: Scaling Mineral Extraction
2025-2027.description: Scaling up mineral extraction from high-value licensed areas with documented in-situ resources, supported by internal and external geological analysis.

2025-2029-1.title: Geothermal Power Expansion
2025-2029-1.description: Expanding our geothermal-based power production from 1 TWh to 10 TWh annually, enabling energy-integrated mining and long-term operational stability.

2025-2029-2.title: Circular Resource Utilization
2025-2029-2.description: Advancing environmentally responsible mineral recovery from mine water and tailings, pioneering a new standard in circular resource utilization.

2025-2029-3.title: Strategic Partnerships & Growth
2025-2029-3.description: Strengthening strategic partnerships across energy, industry, and finance to prepare for international growth and selective asset monetization.

projects.title: Our Projects
projects.subtitle: Exploring critical mineral resources across Norway's most promising regions

portfolio.title: Exploration Portfolio
portfolio.subtitle: Our strategic portfolio of critical mineral exploration projects across Norway's most promising geological regions

snoefjell.description: 45 drillcores document 350,000 tonnes of ore in 5 meter height, with an average of 1.8% Beryllium. Contains very interesting values for light REEs including Rubidium (Rb), Niobium (Nb), Zirconium (Zr), Uranium (U), and Yttrium (Y).
snoefjell.overview: Snøfjell Deposit represents one of the most significant Rare Earth Element discoveries in Norway. 45 drillcores document 350,000 tonnes of ore in 5 meter height, with an average of 1.8% Beryllium content. The deposit shows exceptional concentrations of light Rare Earth Elements.
snoefjell.coverage: 350,000 Tonnes Ore Documented | 270 km² Licensed Area

malmfosdalen.description: Historic mining district (1906-1997) producing iron, copper, and sulfur. Home to the revolutionary Mine Water Resource Project. We have identified viable deposits of Cerium at levels never observed in Norway before, along with Gold, Zinc, Copper, Nickel, and Cobalt.
malmfosdalen.overview: The Malm Mine District represents 20,000 man-years of mining history dating back to 1906. Originally operated by Fosdalens Bergverks A/S, the mines produced over 35 million tonnes of ore during 1906-1997, yielding nearly 16 million tonnes of magnetite concentrate. The district is now the site of our innovative Mine Water Resource Project, revolutionizing mineral extraction.

lergruvbakken.description: A Pyrite-Zinc deposit with original reserves of ~940,000 tonnes. Primary commodities include Zinc (5.5%), Copper (0.45%), Lead (0.25%), and Silver (10 ppm). Mining took place in the 1970s and ceased in 1977.
lergruvbakken.overview: Lergruvbakken is part of the historic Røros mining district and was one of the later small underground mines. Mining at Lergruvbakken took place in the 1970s and ceased when the company went into bankruptcy in 1977. The deposit is described in NGU records as a pyrite-zinc type deposit, with zinc as the primary commodity.
lergruvbakken.coverage: $376 Million USD Indicated Value | 52,200 m² Regulated Area

methodology.title: Resource Valuation Methodology
methodology.subtitle: How we calculate our resource estimates and valuations
methodology.description: The in-situ resource estimates presented by Norve'Ge Minerals AS are based on a combination of internal and external assessments. Three experienced in-house geologists, together with one external consultant, have reviewed geological data from the Norwegian Geological Survey (NGU), the national mining registry (Dirmin.no), and historical production reports. Inferred volumes have been modeled using borehole results, geophysical datasets, and conservative assumptions based on documented mineral concentrations. All values represent preliminary in-situ valuations and are intended as indicative figures for exploration and development planning.

investment.question: How can I invest in NORVEGE MINERALS AS?
investment.answer: Please contact us through the contact form or email us directly at contact@norvegeminerals.no. Our investor relations team will provide you with detailed information about investment opportunities.

location.question: Where are your exploration projects located?
location.answer: Our projects are located across Norway, including regions in Sogn og Fjordane, Telemark, Trøndelag, and Finnmark. Visit our Projects page for detailed information about each project.

minerals.question: What minerals are you exploring for?
minerals.answer: We focus on critical minerals essential for the energy transition, including copper, zinc, nickel, cobalt, rare earth elements, and gold.

sustainability.question: How do you ensure sustainable and responsible mining?
sustainability.answer: Sustainability is at the core of our operations. We follow strict environmental standards, engage with local communities, and implement best practices in ESG. Visit our ESG page for more details.

visits.question: Can I visit your exploration sites?
visits.answer: For safety and operational reasons, exploration sites are generally not open to public visits. However, we regularly engage with local communities and stakeholders. Please contact us if you have specific inquiries.

updates.question: How can I stay updated on your progress?
updates.answer: You can subscribe to our newsletter through the contact form, follow us on social media, or check our website regularly for updates and reports.

esg.title: ESG & Sustainability
esg.subtitle: Our Commitment to Responsible Mining
esg.heroSubtitle: Responsible mining is not optional—it's fundamental to everything we do

commitment.title: Our Commitment
commitment.description1: At NORVEGE MINERALS AS, we recognize that the minerals we explore are essential for the global energy transition. However, we also understand that how we extract these resources matters just as much as what we extract. We are committed to the highest standards of environmental stewardship, social responsibility, and corporate governance.
commitment.description2: Our operations align with Norwegian regulations, EU standards, and international best practices for responsible mining.

landUse.title: Land Use & Biodiversity
landUse.description: We conduct comprehensive environmental impact assessments before any exploration activity. Our goal is to minimize disturbance to ecosystems and protect biodiversity in the areas where we operate.

waterManagement.title: Water Management
waterManagement.description: Protecting water resources is a top priority. We implement strict water management protocols to prevent contamination and ensure sustainable use.

emissions.title: Emissions & Climate
emissions.description: We track and work to minimize our carbon footprint, recognizing the irony of extracting minerals for the energy transition while contributing to emissions. Our exploration methods prioritize low-impact techniques.

communities.title: Local Communities
communities.description: We engage with local communities early and often, ensuring that our activities are transparent and that community concerns are heard and addressed.

indigenousRights.title: Indigenous Rights
indigenousRights.description: We respect the rights of indigenous peoples, including the Sami, and ensure that our operations do not infringe on traditional lands or cultural heritage.

jobsSafety.title: Jobs & Safety
jobsSafety.description: We prioritize the health and safety of our workers and aim to create quality employment opportunities in the regions where we operate.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.description: We maintain open and transparent reporting on our activities, financials, and ESG performance. Stakeholders have a right to know how we operate.

compliance.title: Regulatory Compliance
compliance.description: We adhere to all Norwegian mining regulations and work closely with regulatory authorities to ensure full compliance.

ethics.title: Ethical Business Practices
ethics.description: Our business is conducted with integrity, honesty, and respect for all stakeholders. We have zero tolerance for corruption or unethical behavior.

partners.title: Strategic Partners
partners.subtitle: Strategic partnerships for sustainable mineral development
partners.description: NORVEGE MINERALS AS collaborates with strategic partners to advance our exploration and development activities. Our partnerships span public institutions, municipalities, and strategic development organizations.
partners.ctaDescription: We are always interested in exploring new partnership opportunities that align with our values and strategic objectives. If you are interested in partnering with us, please contact us through our contact form.
